title: REST API pull doesn't work behind corporate proxy
body:

The issue described in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8908 is still happening on my system.

podman pull iad.ocir.io/iotdev/edge_stg/edge-agent:0.5.0 works

however podman pull through curl gets stuck...

[root@localhost systemd]# !299
curl  -X POST --unix-socket  /run/user/2002/podman/podman.sock -v http://d/v4.1.1/libpod/images/pull?reference=iad.ocir.io/iotdev/edge_stg/edge-agent:0.5.0
* Uses proxy env variable no_proxy == 'localhost,127.0.0.1,.us.oracle.com,.oraclecorp.com,.in.oracle.com,.oraclevpn.com'
* Uses proxy env variable http_proxy == 'http://www-proxy.us.oracle.com:80'
*   Trying /run/user/2002/podman/podman.sock:0...
* Connected to d (/run/user/2002/podman/podman.sock) port 80 (#0)
> POST /v4.1.1/libpod/images/pull?reference=iad.ocir.io/iotdev/edge_stg/edge-agent:0.5.0 HTTP/1.1
> Host: d
> User-Agent: curl/7.76.1
> Accept: */*
>
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Api-Version: 1.41
< Libpod-Api-Version: 4.2.0
< Server: Libpod/4.2.0 (linux)
< X-Reference-Id: 0xc0006496d0
< Date: Thu, 11 May 2023 22:10:17 GMT
< Transfer-Encoding: chunked
<
{"stream":"Trying to pull iad.ocir.io/iotdev/edge_stg/edge-agent:0.5.0...\n"}

...

Tried podman system service --timeout 0& and running the curl command with same output




### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. curl  -X POST --unix-socket  /run/user/2002/podman/podman.sock -v http://d/v4.1.1/libpod/images/pull?reference=iad.ocir.io/iotdev/edge_stg/edge-agent:0.5.0 doesn't work behind corporate proxy
2. podman system service --timeout 0 after setting the environment variables doesn't work either
3.


### Describe the results you received

Unable to connect

### Describe the results you expected

Pull container

### Upstream Latest Release

comments.body:
I noticed that inspite of starting posman system service --timeout 0 manually , after curl is done it starts another process /usr/bin/podman --log-level=info system service

I suspect that process is not passed env variables

It sounds like you have a podman.socket or podman.service listening.  Make sure you shut it down.

systemctl --user stop podman.socket podman.service

No they were stopped . What I observed is that when curl command is issued another podman process is started even though "podman system service --timeout 0 " is already running.

That process seem to process the API call . 

For now we fixed the issue by manually inserting the proxy variables in :  /usr/share/containers/containers.conf 

1.Need to add http_proxy, https_proxy in the env section
env = [
"https_proxy=[http://my proxy)",
"http_proxy=[http://my proxy/)"
]
2. Set env_host to true
  env_host = true
3. set http_proxy https_proxy to true
  http_proxy = true
  https_proxy = true

If another process is launched, that means that podman.socket is still being socket activated by systemd.

You should make those changes in /etc/containers/containers.conf,  The next update can replace /usr/share/containers/containers.conf.

Looks like we're good to close the issue.

As @rhatdan mentioned, make sure to create a config in `/etc/containers/containers.conf` instead.  `/usr/share` is reserved to packages, so content may be lost on updates.
